entry.title: Re: Rewiring of SAP Datasphere to SAP Business Data Cloud
entry.content: Thanks for the clarification — this ‘rewiring’ concept is already causing a lot of questions in the field because it sounds technical but is largely a commercial/entitlement transition rather than a data or object migration. As many of us are looking at how Datasphere fits into broader platform strategies, I’d be curious to hear your thoughts on two aspects: how rewiring impacts governance and lifecycle tooling across hyperscalers and geo-regions, and how it aligns with BDC’s multi-component architecture (e.g., Datasphere, BDC foundation services, and SAP Databricks) especially when customers plan to blend SAP and non-SAP data. Interested in any architectural patterns or lessons learned you’ve seen in these scenarios.

entry.title: Re: New Community Package: A Cloud Integration Alternative for SAP Process Orchestration Adapter Mod
entry.content:
Thanks for putting this new community package together — this is very practical for teams migrating from SAP Process Orchestration to Cloud Integration, especially since many of the traditional adapter modules (e.g., MessageTransformBean, XMLAnonymizer, IDoc/XML converters) are not directly available in Cloud Integration out of the box. The scripts and sample flows clearly lower the friction for transformation and format handling in real projects. I’d be curious to understand how you’d recommend structuring these scripts and flows in a large, regulated enterprise landscape — should they be packaged as reusable library artifacts across subaccounts and environments, and how do you think this approach compares with using API-centric transformation services or SAP Integration Suite’s partner ecosystem tools in complex pipelines?” 

 

Question:

“the context of overall migration strategy from PI/PO to SAP Integration Suite, how do you see this community package fitting with the broader pipeline/migration patterns SAP is promoting (e.g., the Cloud Integration pipeline concept)? Would you advise combining this package with pipeline templates to preserve orchestration semantics and partner/receiver determination behavior from PI/PO

We are excited to launch this space where learning, collaboration, and real-world experience come together. It’s a space where ideas grow, questions are welcome, and learning becomes a shared journey instead of a solo effort. Whether you ask questions, share insights, or simply follow along and learn at your own pace, you are part of this community.

Let’s connect, learn, and grow together. We look forward to getting to know you.

entry.title: What’s New in SAP Cell and Gene Therapy Orchestration - Release 2511
entry.content:
Introduction

The SAP Cell and Gene Therapy Orchestration (CGTO) solution continues to evolve with every release, strengthening orchestration, compliance, and integration of capabilities across the cell and gene therapy lifecycle. With Release 2511, SAP delivers a comprehensive set of innovations across scheduling, labeling, supply chain automation, compliance, and EWM integration - all focused on supporting scalable and compliant personalized therapy operations.‑therapy operations.

This blog summarizes the key enhancements from 2511 Release

Introducing Treatment Scheduling

Treatment Scheduling is the headline capability of Release 2511, establishing the foundational layer for end-to-end orchestration around manufacturing slot availability and milestone-based planning.

  • Definition and management of Manufacturing Slots: Administrators can configure slot definitions per plant, material, and therapy, including daily capacities.
  • Milestones & Lead Times Framework: CGTO now generates standard milestones (e.g., biospecimen pickup, delivery, processing start/end, finished product pickup/delivery) and enables maintaining milestone sequencing and lead times.
  • Scheduling Engine: The new engine calculates multiday schedules and proposes multiple date combinations based on :
    • Available slot capacity
    • Process milestones
    • Lead times between milestones
  • Soft Booking & Hard Booking:
    • Soft bookings block capacity temporarily.
    • Hard bookings are confirmed after the schedule is approved.
  • Auto‑blocking subsequent slots for multi‑day processing windows.
  • Support for multi plant workflows, where manufacturing steps occur at different locations.

Enhanced Labeling Framework

The labeling capability has been completely modernized to support extensibility, configurability, and industry standards.

  • Configurable Label Types for any CGTO entity: Treatment Order, Shipment, Materials, Sub‑units, etc.
  • Flexible Label Data Collection: Data can be pulled from CGTO and extended through an enhancement point.
  • Configurable Label Templates usable across multiple label types.
  • Automatic generation of label line items when the corresponding CGTO entities are created.
  • Full audit trail and e-signature support, aligned with GxP expectations.
  • Other functionalities like View Labels, Generate, Reprint, Invalidate, Preview, continue to be supported

The legacy label apps remain fully supported through backward compatibility.

Flexible Material Determination

To support broader customer scenarios, CGTO leverages Business Rule Framework for determining materials across: Biospecimen Shipments, Biospecimen Kit Shipments and Finished Product Shipments. The new enhancement now allows the material determination based on :

    • Order header attributes
    • Shipment data
    • Material group/type
    • Additional configurable fields

This provides customers with significantly greater control over how materials are derived for each therapy and process step. It is backward compatible via a feature flag.

Business Partner Function Determination

CGTO now supports automatic determination of Partner Functions (Sold-to, Bill-to, Ship-to, Vendor, etc.) based on configuration.

  • When business partners are created via events or UI, the correct function is pre‑assigned.
  • Users can override partner functions manually when needed.

Subsequent Finished Product Transit Leg Creation

When a new Finished Product shipment is created after the flow version has been confirmed, CGTO now automatically creates the corresponding transit leg shipment if configured in the corresponding Flow version. This aligns with real operational scenarios where additional doses or repeated shipments are required.

New Treatment Order Summary (Single View Dashboard)‑View Dashboard)

A highly requested feature - a single consolidated view of key data required by supply chain coordinators. Below is a subset of available fields on one screen:

  • Milestone plan dates
  • Material quantities & dosage information
  • Courier details (e.g., waybill numbers)
  • ERP document numbers (e.g., Purchase Order, Sales Order, Process Order, Production Order and Stock Transport Order)
  • Shipment statuses and relevant progress indicators

Transport Management – View Imported Records

To simplify configuration transport between tenants, Release 2511 provides:

  • A consolidated "View Imported Records" page in Manage Transport
  • Ability to view exactly which configuration entities and records were imported
  • Eliminates the need to check each configuration UI separately
  • Strongly improves auditability and change traceability

COI Enhancements – System of Record & Additional Fields

Chain of Identity (COI) is central to CGTO compliance. Enhancements introduced include:

  • New field: Source System ID
    Indicates where each COI event originated
    • CGTO internal updates identify the BTP tenant
    • External updates (TCP, S/4HANA, middleware) pass their own source ID
  • Additional data elements, including donor ID, expiration details for biospecimen collection batches

Embedded Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) Integration for Goods Movements

One of the biggest integration deliveries in 2511is native integration with Embedded EWM. CGTO now receives and orchestrates EWM based goods movements for:

  • Purchase Orders (PGR in EWM updates Biospecimen shipment)
  • Process Orders (PGI/PGR update processing activity)
  • Stock Transport Orders (PGI/PGR update intermediate & final leg shipments) leg shipments)‑leg shipments)
  • Sales Orders (PGI updates FP shipments)
  • Stock Postings (via EWM)

Key benefits:

  • Full traceability of handling units
  • Automatic updating of sub‑units in CGTO
  • Improved synchronization with warehouse operations

COI Validations in SAP S/4HANA

To ensure end-to-end identity compliance, CGTO now enables SAP S/4HANA to validate the COI identifier across the following documents. The COI, captured as batch characteristics from the batch used in the SAP S/4HANA document, is validated against the COI ID from the respective order.

Purchase Orders

  • During PO create/update
  • During inbound delivery create/update
  • During Post Goods Receipt (PGR)

Process Orders

  • Validate COI during order create/update
  • Validate COI for input materials during consumption (PGI)
  • Validate COI for output batches during yield (PGR)

STO & Sales Orders

  • Validate COI during SO/OBD/PGI operations

Each validation ensures that only batches with matching COI identifiers progress through the ERP process — protecting patient safety.

Transfer Postings via MIGO

Release 2511 extends COI compliant orchestration to MIGO transfer postings. Supported stock type transitions include:

  • Unrestricted ↔ Blocked
  • Blocked ↔ Quality Inspection
  • Blocked ↔ Unrestricted
  • Unrestricted ↔ Quality Inspection

CGTO receives disposition events for each transfer when the user enters COI data on the MIGO screen.

Conclusion

Release 2511 represents a significant milestone in the evolution of SAP Cell and Gene Therapy Orchestration.
From advanced scheduling to deeper ERP/EWM integration, enhanced labeling, and stronger identity compliance, the enhancements in this release deliver both operational efficiency and regulatory assurance — exactly what personalized therapy supply chains need today.

If you're evaluating or implementing CGTO, these innovations enable faster adoption, stronger process control, and a path toward highly scalable CGT operations.

entry.title: AI Is Transforming Sales Order Processing in SAP
entry.content:
Challenges in Manual Sales Order Processing

  • Inconsistent customer data: duplicate addresses, incorrect zip codes, or special characters.
  • Complex Sold-to / Ship-to relationships: a single customer may have multiple shipping addresses.
  • Manual verification of POs is time-consuming and prone to human error.

Ensuring timely delivery and customer satisfaction requires high workforce effort

AI & Machine Learning Workflow

The AI-assisted SAP workflow automates the sales order creation process as follows:

Step 1: Customer sends an email with a Purchase Order (PO).
Step 2: AI email reader parses the email.
Step 3: OCR and NLP extract the PO details.
Step 4: ML identifies the correct Sold-to / Ship-to relationship, even in cases of inconsistent or duplicate data.
Step 5: Data validation checks accuracy against authoritative sources (e.g., USPS address verification).
Step 6: SAP automatically creates the sales order.
Step 7: Customer receives order confirmation.

AI and ML pipeline automates PO processing, resolves Sold-to/Ship-to relationships, validates data, and creates sales orders in SAP.

Key Benefits

  • Increased Productivity: Automates repetitive manual tasks.
  • Reduced Errors: Correctly identifies Sold-to / Ship-to and validates data.
  • Time Savings: Faster order processing and reduced cycle time.
  • Enhanced Customer Satisfaction: Timely, accurate orders and confirmations.
  • Cost Efficiency: Reduces workforce dependency and operational costs.

Machine Learning for Sold-to / Ship-to Resolution

The ML system analyzes historical sales data and learns patterns to:

  • Resolve duplicate or inconsistent addresses.
  • Identify correct shipping addresses for multiple “Sold-to” customers.
  • Handle special characters and local language variations.
  • Ensure accurate SAP sales order creation.

This step is critical because errors in Sold-to / Ship-to mapping can delay shipments, cause billing issues, and reduce customer satisfaction

Integration with SAP

  • AI/ML connects via REST APIs or SAP AI Services.
  • Extracted and validated PO data is sent directly to SAP ERP, eliminating manual entry.
  • SAP automatically generates sales orders, streamlines accounting, and updates inventory.

Conclusion

Integrating AI and ML into SAP sales order processing transforms a manual, error-prone process into an efficient, automated workflow. By resolving customer data issues, identifying correct Sold-to / Ship-to relationships, and validating PO information, organizations can:

  • Enhance operational efficiency
  • Reduce costs
  • Improve customer satisfaction
  • Focus human resources on higher-value tasks

entry.title: Announcement: Register to the new CEI projects for Master Data in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
In 2026, we start the year with multiple Customer Engagement Initiative (CEI) projects for Master Data in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

We will collaborate with our customers and partners of the master objects Business Partner and Product Master  to gather feedback on product improvements, enhancements, and new developments to validate our planned enhancements at an early stage, and ensure that what we build meets your business needs

If you would like to join the upcoming CEI projects to provide feedback and shape the future of Business Partner and Product Master, here are the projects that are open for registration now:

  • Material Long Texts – Automated Content Generation for Product Master
  • Business Partner and Product Master in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • SAP S/4HANA Business Partner Master Data Analytics Dashboard
  • Product Insights / Product 360

Registration is open from the 9th of February 2026 until the 13th of March 2026.

After the registration closes, we will invite all registered participants for an initial call to explain the scope and prerequisites of the project. This will be around early April.

Later, we will run dedicated feedback sessions with each registered participant. After we collate feedback from all registered participants, we will host a closing call to summarize our learnings, review the feedback collected, and discuss next steps.

Register today to ensure you do not miss this opportunity to influence and shape the future of Master Data in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition to meet your business needs. 

We look forward to your active participation and valuable feedback.

entry.title: Re: How to do Batch call in RAP Odata with CHANGE_SET ena...
entry.content: In RAP, multiple operations must be grouped under a single changeset within the $batch call to ensure they are processed as one atomic Logical Unit of Work(All-or-nothing). Because the RAP runtime handles the DB commits automatically, there is no need to explicitly implement defer mode or redefine changeset methods as you would in a classic DPC_EXT class.

entry.title: Re: UI5 MessageStrip - Assertion failed: Method 'class' m...
entry.content:
Was resolved. Issue was not within the class attribute. It was within the "visible" attribute.

See UI5 MessageStrip Error: Assertion failed: Method '... - SAP Community for details

entry.title: Re: UI5 MessageStrip Error: Assertion failed: Method 'cla...
entry.content:
Hi,
 
@Yogananda, @Filipp thank you for your answers.
 
I thought, that I went through all scenarios. Like removing the MessageStrips in all views and just focus on one. But as Yogananda wrote, that it worked, I went back to triple check. 
 
It took me a while to find something strange. Mea Culpa.
 
I pasted Yoganandas Code next to the other commended MessageStrip, and the error was still there. 
 
The faulty code (of a test project, that nobody would ever see in production, and this editor won't let me highligt it) : 
 
<MessageStrip 
class="sapUiSmallMargin" 
text="Blah" 
showIcon="true" 
visible="{=(${ODataThing>ODataAttr1}.length === 0) ? true : false}" />
 
Basically, if I put the a MessageStrip tag into all places. 
 
But why does it happen? 

The whole structure is a floating content layout with two columns. The 1st column is a list, that 'loads' details into the 2nd column. The 2nd column consists of an ObectPageLayout. The faulty code lives in a fragment (1Fragment) inside of one of the subSections.

<sections>
<ObjectPageSection title="..." id="1Section">
<subSections>
<ObjectPageSubSection id="1SubSection"
visible="{= !${...} }">
<blocks>
<core:Fragment fragmentName="1Fragment" type="XML"/>
</blocks>
</ObjectPageSubSection>
</subSections>

But not only there. I've also got a Wizard in a Dialog fragment, which is triggered on a button from the 1st column. That has MessageStrip tags as well. Same issue with them. 

The question is more, why does it fail when rendering the MessageStrip? Could it be another problem? 

The error message states, that the class method must be called with exactly one class name. If this is referring to CSS classes... It still happens if I remove multiple CSS classes entirely. 



BR

entry.title: 🇪🇸 Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core (Madrid, Spain)
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam event is on Getting Started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core and Python. You do not need any background in the topic, but you should have a lot of curiosity!

In this CodeJam, you will learn how to use the Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core to implement retrieval-augmented generation (RAG). This approach improves the responses of large language models (LLMs) and reduces hallucinations. You will learn how to deploy an LLM and the orchestration service on SAP AI Core, and how to query it via SAP AI Launchpad and the Python SDK. Additionally, you will gain insights into key generative AI concepts and learn how to enhance RAG responses.

This is a free in-person event and is planned for Friday, March 20, 2026 in Madrid from 09:30 to 14:00. The hands-on content is in English but the language of the event will be mainly in Spanish.

Thanks to UC3M for sponsoring the event.

The instructors is SAP Developer Advocate @ajmaradiaga!

Location: Aula 10.0.27, Universidad Carlos III de Madrid | Campus de Getafe, Calle Madrid, Getafe, Spain

Please note that spaces are limited; we can only accommodate 15 for external attendees as some space is dedicated for students. If you wish to attend, register by clicking the Yes button in the Will you be attending? section above to the right. A few weeks before the event, we will ask you to confirm your attendance.

Prerequisites: Any Chromium-based web browser, such as Microsoft Edge, Google Chrome etc.

Visit the SAP CodeJam group for more information on SAP CodeJam events, to ask questions, and to chat with your fellow attendees.

entry.title: The Combined Power of SAP Joule and AI for the RISE with SAP Methodology
entry.content:
Part five of the five-part series, “RISE with SAP Methodology”

AI is everywhere right now. Not just in tech headlines or innovation labs, but also in boardrooms, architecture discussions, and project plans. And if you work with SAP solutions, you’ve likely noticed that most road maps now mention it, too.

Many of us have seen “the next big thing” come and go. New tools, new buzzwords, and big promises have often delivered limited practical impact. So, the real question isn’t whether AI exists. It’s “where will AI actually make a difference?”

Organizations want technology that helps people work better, make smarter decisions faster and cut through complexity in day-to-day work with real-world pressures and consequential deadlines. AI should not be treated as a standalone innovation topic, but as a capability embedded across products, processes and user experiences.

That’s exactly the direction SAP’s new strategy is taking. At the heart of this strategy sits a simple idea: bring applications, data, and AI together in SAP Business Suite, so intelligence becomes part of how work naturally happens, as shown in Figure 1.

Figure 1: The Future of AI in SAP Business Suite

SAP Joule vs. AI Capabilities: Understanding the Difference

When people talk about AI in SAP environments, two concepts often get mixed together: AI capabilities and the SAP Joule solution.

While connected, each one plays vastly distinct roles:

  • AI capabilities form the foundation and are the engine, quietly running in the background. Powerful, but often invisible, they analyze huge volumes of data, predict risks and generate recommendations. They include technologies such as machine learning, predictive analytics and automation embedded across SAP solutions.

  • SAP Joule, on the other hand, is the interaction layer between people and AI and facilitates one-on-one conversations with the user. This is the moment when intelligence becomes accessible. Instead of searching through lots of guidelines, dashboards or compiling reports, users simply ask questions, such as “What should I focus on next in our transformation project?” or “Where are we off track?”. Users can even just say “Summarize the risks”. And in return, they get answers immediately and in context. (see Figure 2 below)

Together, AI capabilities and SAP Joule form a complementary model where AI feels natural, not abstract. The experience is remarkably similar to talking to a knowledgeable colleague who already understands the system landscape. There’s deep intelligence behind the scenes and a simple, human interaction up front.

Figure 2: Interacting with SAP Joule as the new AI user experience

SAP Joule: A practical entry point to AI

As shown in Figure 2 above, one of SAP´s strategic priority is to offer a new AI user experience through SAP Joule. This AI-enabled assistant introduces a simple, practical way to bring AI into transformation work.

While delivering many AI capabilities across the SAP solution portfolio, SAP Joule goes beyond using AI as another analytics tool or dashboard by running an interaction layer that allows customers to work with AI through a single, conversational interface.

This changes how teams engage with information during transformation projects or in day-to-day business. Instead of navigating multiple tools, reports, or status decks, users can interact with SAP Joule in natural language—asking questions, requesting summaries, or starting conversations on how to proceed with tasks alongside the transformation project. (see Figure 3)

Behind the scenes, SAP Joule connects to relevant data across SAP solutions and presents answers in a way that is easy to understand and easy to act on. In practice, this lowers the barrier to insight. Instead of searching for information, teams start working interactively with it.

Figure 3: Using SAP Joule interactively during your transformation work

Strategic enablers of the transformation journey

As mentioned before, AI is embedded directly into SAP solutions and workflows to help people navigate complexity. Users can surface relevant insights, identify patterns, and reduce manual effort.

Take, for example, transforming an ERP landscape. This project can easily become complex, depending on customer requirements. The volume of information that teams must use at the same time—including process data, system insights, project status, testing results, risks, and dependencies—often spread across tools and documents. Even with a clear methodology in place, the effort required to connect these dots manually has become a challenge of its own.

RISE with SAP Methodology already provides the structure needed by providing the integrated tooling, standardized framework, and expert guidance to support the proper execution discipline. However, embedding AI in the methodology adds a new layer of intelligence at the point of action, helping teams understand what matters where risks are emerging, and which decisions requires attention (see Figure 4).

In this case, AI reduces the load that comes with large-scale change. When applied correctly, it supports people in making better and faster decisions, especially when time and attention are limited.

Meanwhile, SAP Joule is used whenever clarity is needed (see Figure 4). This opens up a new way of working with guidance, insights, and execution support throughout the transformation journey when using RISE with SAP Methodology, serving as an active companion.

Figure 4: SAP Joule and AI as an integral part of RISE with SAP Methodology

Giving an end-to-end perspective alongside the transformation, SAP Joule allows project teams to handle time-consuming and complex tasks faster and with greater ease. This includes searching for information and guidelines that can help them start and review extensive assessments and analyses.

As planning and design activities progress, SAP Joule enables teams to stay oriented. It supports prioritization, highlights dependencies, and helps validate assumptions before decisions become difficult to reverse. AI does not replace workshops or experience, but it strengthens them with data-driven insight.

During execution, when the build and testing phases accelerate, SAP Joule supports transparency and focus. AI capabilities detect patterns in defects, progress, and quality signals, while SAP Joule summarizes what matters most. So instead of consolidating information manually, teams can react early.

SAP Joule supports readiness discussions as go-live approaches. By correlating information across testing, migration, and operational checks, AI helps teams assess whether they are truly ready based on evidence, not gut feeling.

After go-live, AI continues to support continuous improvement. Usage patterns, incidents, and performance signals are analyzed in the background. SAP Joule helps connect teams to those insights to understand where to improve next and how operational issues relate back to earlier transformation decisions.

Practical value over automation

The strength of AI in SAP transformations does not lie in automation alone.
It lies in reducing all the cognitive load that happens during the transformation project.

By combining all elements of the RISE with SAP Methodology:

  • a clear path (with the Standardized Framework)
  • Integrated tooling (with the Integrated toolchain)
  • End-to-end Execution support (with the Expert Guidance) and
  • AI-driven assistance through SAP Joule and specific AI capabilities

customers are well equipped to move through their transformation with focus, confidence and consistency.

AI does not replace people or experience.
It supports both – at the right moment, in the right context.

To learn more about RISE with SAP Methodology, read our in-depth overview.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

RISE with SAP Methodology - Blog-series 

Discover the 5-part blog series:

  • Blog 1/5 (December 3rd, 2025)
    RISE with SAP Methodology: A practical Guide to Modernizing with Confidence
  • Blog 2/5 (December 10th, 2025)
    Deep Dive: Inside the framework of RISE with SAP Methodology
  • Blog 3/5 (January 14th, 2026)
    The Integrated Toolchain in a Nutshell
  • Blog 4/5 (January 28th, 2026)
    Expert Guidance: Your Guided Transformation Accelerator
  • Blog 5/5 (February 11th, 2026)
    The Combined Power of SAP Joule and AI for the RISE with SAP Methodology

 --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

entry.title: Create Your Own SAP BDC Workshop: Academy Workbook
entry.content:
As SAP Partners, we often need to demonstrate the power of the SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) to customers. However, setting up a multi-user environment from scratch can be daunting.

Fortunately, the SAP PartnerEdge Academy allows you to provision a dedicated, pre-configured workshop environment. Whether you are running a specific customer opportunity or a broader training event, this guide will walk you through the setup process, best practices for user management, and the content your attendees will experience.

Guide to the Academy Workbook

Part 1: Access and Registration: To get started, you need to book the environment through the Partner Portal.

Navigate to Partner Academies page here https://partneredge.sap.com/en/products/btp/about/academies.html and Login. 

Scroll down the page until you see the section for "SAP Business Data Cloud" or navigate to direct link.

Start Registration: Click on the "Academy Registration".

⚠️Troubleshooting Tip: Some partners have reported receiving an "Internal Server Error" when clicking the registration link. If this happens to you, please open a Private / Incognito window in your browser and try accessing the link again.

Part 2: Configuring Your Workshop

Once inside the registration tool, accuracy is key to ensuring you get the right support.

Select the Workbook: Check the box for "SAP Business Data Cloud" and click on Go To Next Step.

Enter Workshop Details: Fill in the required fields with specific details like Customer Name, Description, CRM Number.

Planned Dates: Select your start and end dates (maximum 30 days)

Engagement Type:

  • Set this to “Self Education” for learning purpose.
  • Or set this to "Workshop" for customer engagement. Add Number of Expected End Users upto 99 users(Be realistic about the headcount)

Preferred Data Center (Select the nearest to customer to ensure low ping)

Description & Support Context: In the description field, be as specific as possible. Mention the Customer Name, the specific Opportunity or the Event Name (e.g., "BDC Workshop Phase 1").  Why? This information helps SAP support assist you much faster if you run into technical issues during your event.

Finalize: Click "Save Planned Workshop Configuration".

Part 3: The Logistics (only for Workshop scheduling)

If you had requested for Workshop to multiple users, you will receive the User Range (e.g: User01 – User50) and Password via email and in your event dashboard.

User Mapping best practice: The tool provides generic user IDs, but it does not email your attendees automatically. Mapping these generic IDs to actual people is your responsibility.

The Challenge: You must assign User01 to Alice, User02 to Bob, etc., and securely share the password with them.

The Recommendation: Do not do this manually for large groups. Use a Mail Merge tools or a workflow automation tool (like Airtable or Smartsheet) to map email addresses to User IDs.

Timing: Send these credentials to your attendees before the workshop begins and ask them to test their login. This ensures your actual workshop time is spent on learning, not troubleshooting passwords.

Part 4: The Workshop Content

Once you or your attendees log in, will be greeted by the SAP Business Data Cloud Cockpit. Here is the flow to follow:

Locate the Guide: Select "Workbooks" > Click "SAP Business Data Cloud" > Click "Start Workbook".

The workbook includes the following chapters:

  • Getting Started with SAP Business Data Cloud
  • Install Intelligent Application (Content Network)
  • Consume Intelligent Application
  • Integrate SAP Databricks Capabilities (Lakehouse integration)
  • Data Modeling in SAP Datasphere
  • Customize Application in SAP Analytics Cloud
  • BW Modernization

Summary

By utilizing the PartnerEdge Academy, you can access the SAP Business Data Cloud Workbook for Self Education.

Or you can deliver a high-quality, hands-on experience without the infrastructure overhead and you will be set for a successful Workshop!

SAP Business Data Cloud  Partnership

entry.title: Re: SAP HANA Rebates: Partner determination for Contract...
entry.content:
Good day @mirthalalozanocorrea 

The simple answer is No. Not all invoices will be considered by the system if you switch partner determination to SH and the billing documents do not carry Ship-To in your business volume relevant data.

The reason for that is, Settlement reads your business volume based on the partner fields available in your source billing data and criteria you have selected for the contract. If you have not defined Ship-To in that contract documents, then those invoices will not match a Ship-To based contract selection.

Here is what to consider before you change to SH:

  1. Check your sample billing documents and ensure that Ship-To is available in your relevant partner data.
  2. Run a SH test with one contract and verify the document produces the right hit list.
  3. If the system does not consistently populate Ship-To, then keep the current invoicing-party based logic. You may also consider redesigning source process so Ship-To to make it always available.

So, in summary, my recommendation is:

  • Do not switch to SH globally until you validate partner availability in billing volume data.
  • Ensure you carry out a pilot contract and settlement simulation to confirm what the system does.


Consult the following references for more insights:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/ee9ee0ca4c3942068ea584d2f929b5b1/5404fc6d6c624c3f960a5a94eca578a4.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/83bc764dd7874d349cc8150bdab246f6/8cca5958d916a007e10000000a441470.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/83bc764dd7874d349cc8150bdab246f6/a9479b52999f46258b48db0c7a63c956.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/83bc764dd7874d349cc8150bdab246f6/0465ed12d12d460298848d82b84db98d.html?locale=en-US

https://learning.sap.com/videos/settlement-of-sales-rebate-agreements-sap-s-4hana-cloud-for-sales-public-edition

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Clean Core in 2026: Can We Truly Achieve It Without a BTP‑First Mindset?
entry.content:
Is Clean Core achievable without shifting custom logic, governance, and integration to BTP?

Of course it can, BTP is just a tool, yet another tool, not the Holly Grail of S/4 implementations

 

What are the biggest blockers you’ve seen?

Missing functionality in standard.

 

Are customers still customizing the core because of regulatory pressure, timelines, or lack of BTP skills?

Core is customized when standard functionality doesn't meet business requirements - as simple as that. Not all enhancements/customizations can be offloaded to BTP, for instance just from TM space:

  • missing containers integration in TM-EWM interface
  • delivery dates for freight orders with statistical stages - see my blog Hyperspeed planning in SAP TM for details

entry.title: Re: Italian special depreciation keys for ledger 2L
entry.content:
Good day @javierflorez 

Thank you for your question.

The simple answer to your question on extending the keys to 2L is NO.

The reason is, since 103 and 138 are delivered as Italy GAAP specific keys for depreciation, you cannot directly reuse them for 2L, when IFRS does not allow them.

Instead of trying to extend 2L, I suggest:

  1. You use an IFRS-eligible depreciation key for the 2L depreciation area, or
  2. You may create or copy an equivalent key in the allowed configuration scope for your chart of depreciation and assign it to the 2L area. However, this will be dependent on your SSCUI tenant permission.

In summary, standard principle is that, depreciation keys are provided based on each countries accounting principle and you cannot freely modify or assign them across principle.

Consult the references below for further insights.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/3e5fcf2c768746049b5627bd5a42f720/ca8143c09b35455c98c509b9800dedd5.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/3e5fcf2c768746049b5627bd5a42f720/a19cc2531bb9b44ce10000000a174cb4.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/doc/c92c3e87a7fa4ff18b22ec6d5ed1f705/2305/en-US/024dec9ad6c24781848ff2b919a6cc23.pdf

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Vibe Coding with Joule Studio: Bringing Your Own Model with NVIDIA NIM
entry.content:
As we build the future of enterprise AI at SAP, our vision for Joule Studio is clear: to provide a platform where you can build, manage, and scale intelligent agents that are deeply integrated with your business processes. The out-of-the-box capabilities, powered by SAP's native models and the GenAI Hub, are designed for robust, reliable, and context-aware orchestration within the SAP ecosystem. But what if you want more control over how your agent thinks, codes, or reasons? What if the model you want isn’t (yet) available in Joule Studio or SAP GenAI Hub? What if you could build it all using natural language? 

That’s where Bring Your Own Model (BYOM) meets Vibe Coding in Joule Studio.

The Idea: Build a Joule agent powered by external models.

With this example, you can vibe code a custom Joule agent in Joule Studio that uses a Joule skill to call an external AI model like Nemotron hosted via NVIDIA NIM, one of the most powerful open-access foundation models specifically designed to build high-performance, specialized agentic AI systems. 

To be explicit about how this works, it’s a "best of both worlds" approach:

  • The Agent's Core Reasoning: The Joule agent itself continues to leverage its powerful, default Large Language Models for its primary job: reasoning, planning, and orchestrating the overall workflow. The agent's runtime is optimized to perform best with these native models.
  • The Skill's Specialized Task: Then, for a specific task — like integrating with Nvidia’s NIM models in this case — the agent uses a Joule skill to leverage the power of NVIDIA Nemotron model. The skill acts as a bridge to specialized intelligence and deep reasoning. 

Accelerate Agent development with Vibe Coding in Joule Studio 

Vibe coding allows you to transform your ideas into a Joule agents quickly. Here is what you can do with vibe coding in Joule Studio:

  • Build Joule skills with drag and drop simplicity 
  • Build a Joule agent by prompting in natural language. The agent’s expertise and instructions will be filled automatically with SAP best practices
  • Enable the tools that the Agent can utilize to generate an optimized response to the user, including the Joule skill with external model, MCP Server or Document Grounding via AI Core. 
  • Test your agent in the private environment
  • Deploy the agent when ready and share it with colleagues

Vibing Coding in Joule Studio generating Agent's Expertise & Instructions

 After the agent is ready, the conversation flow is simple:

  1. The user interacts with the Joule agent in natural language.
  2. Joule asks the user to choose the Nemotron model. Here, I am using: nvidia/llama-3.1-nemotron-nano-8b-v1 and nvidia/nemotron-3-nano-30b-a3b.
  3. A Joule skill sends the user query and the selected model to the NVIDIA Nemotron API. 
  4. The selected NIM model processes the request and sends the output to the Joule agent.
  5. The Joule agent evaluates the response, checking how the model solved the required task.
  6. The response is then presented to the user.

The key detail: the user can choose which Nemotron model to use, enabling dynamic querying and true BYOM experience.

Joule Runtime conversation: 
"A developer asks Joule to generate a BAPI extension. Nemotron reasons through the logic, generates the code, and explains why it chose that approach."

Joule conversation - User Query, Model Selected & Response from Nemotron.

Model name and response logged in Joule Timeline.

By combining Joule's orchestration with NVIDIA's Nemotron Models, the agent can be scaled to generated responses with deep reasoning, visual contextualization, document analysis and multimodal research. 

Example of a Scenario: The Business Problem
"A procurement manager receives hundreds of POs daily. They need to identify risks, anomalies, and optimization opportunities - but it requires analyzing data across multiple SAP systems + deep reasoning."

The Agentic Flow
Agentic Flow leveraging external Models
Some more examples: 

  • Upload images of defective parts/machinery to identify defects and receive recommendations using multimodal AI. 
  • Analyze invoice PDFs to get explanation of charges and identify missing information using Vision Language models 
  • Accurately forecast customer buying capabilities or inventory fulfilling using predictive models. 

Why Use External Models Like NVIDIA Nemotron?

Using models outside Joule Studio or SAP GenAI Hub unlocks several real-world benefits:

  • Freedom to bring your own model
    Use cutting-edge or domain-specialized models that are not yet natively supported by SAP.
  • Ship Innovative Agents faster
    Experiment with new reasoning, vision, or code-focused models without waiting for platform updates.
  • Choose the right model
    Choose different NIM models for coding, analytics, logistics reasoning, or document understanding.
  • Future-proof architecture
    Your Joule agent becomes model-agnostic, making it easier to swap or upgrade models later.
  • Enterprise-safe orchestration
    Joule Studio still handles identity, governance, and process integrations - only the intelligence is externalized.

Conclusion

You can now build intelligent agents that vibe with your users, adapt on the fly, and leverage state-of-the-art models as of today. Furthermore, your agent can execute at scale to solve more complex problems by empowering them with tools like MCP Server, Document Grounding and Calculators while leveraging the power of external models. 

It’s time to build AI agents that unlock the true potential of your business with tools and models of your choice. Here is how you can get started too: Read the blog here.

entry.title: Powering Event-Driven Integrations Using Amazon EventBridge Adapter in SAP Cloud Integration
entry.content:
INTRODUCTION:

Event-driven architecture is becoming increasingly popular now-a-days due to its efficient real-time data processing, high scalability and better de-coupling of business systems. Hence, it is becoming new favorite for enterprise integration developers.
SAP Integration Suite supports this modern pattern through various adapters, one of which is the Amazon EventBridge receiver adapter.

The Amazon EventBridge receiver adapter allows seamless event publishing from SAP to Amazon EventBridge, enabling SAP applications to interact directly with AWS services such as Lambda, SQS, and SNS without the need for complex custom API developments or integrations.

In this blog, we will explore how to configure and use the Amazon EventBridge receiver adapter to replicate Business Partner data from SAP S/4HANA to Amazon EventBridge in near real time.

What Is Amazon EventBridge?

Amazon EventBridge is a serverless event bus service by AWS that helps connect applications using events.
It allows you to publish events (like new Business Partner creation, Sales Order creation etc) from SAP and trigger AWS services (like Lambda, Step Functions, SNS, or SQS) for further processing.

Using the Amazon EventBridge Adapter in SAP Cloud Integration, you can directly post these events from SAP to an EventBridge event bus without custom coding or API gateway setups.

Business Scenario Overview

In this scenario, an organization maintains Business Partner master data within SAP S/4HANA. Whenever a Business Partner is created or updated, this information needs to be replicated to AWS for further processing such as validation, analytics, or integration with non-SAP applications.

The goal is to enable a real-time, event-driven data flow from SAP to AWS without building or maintaining point-to-point interfaces.

High-level process flow:

  1. A Business Partner is created or updated in SAP S/4HANA.
  2. The change triggers an outbound message, which is sent to SAP Integration Suite.
  3. SAP Cloud Integration processes and transforms the message.
  4. The Amazon EventBridge receiver adapter publishes the event to the configured event bus in Amazon EventBridge.
  5. The event bus routes the event to the respective targets such as AWS Lambda, SQS, or other consumers.

This approach ensures loose coupling, real-time updates, and scalability across systems.

Integration Flow Design in SAP Cloud Integration

Step 1: Sender Configuration

  • Sender Adapter: Sender Adapter: SOA Manager to SAP Integration Suite

To enable sender connectivity from SAP ERP to SAP Integration Suite, SOA manager is used to configure the outbound web service communication. This involves creating and configuring a logical port for the relevant consumer proxy in the ERP system.

The logical port is assigned with the HTTPS endpoint of the SAP CI iFlow, along with the required authentication mechanism (for example, basic authentication or client certificate-based authentication). SSL settings, certificates, and user credentials should be properly maintained to ensure secure communication.

For more information about SOA Manager configuration please visit this page.

Configuring SOA Manager | SAP Help Portal

You can also use any other type of sender configuration such as IDOC.

Step 2: Message Transformation

  • Use a message mapping, groovy script or simple XML to JSON converter to transform the SAP XML payload into the JSON structure as expected by Amazon EventBridge.

Step 3: Receiver Adapter Configuration

  • Receiver Adapter: Use a ‘request reply’ pallet function and connect it to a receiver using Amazon EventBridge receiver adapter. Follow the below steps to configure the adapter.
  • Adapter Configuration Details:
    • AWS Region: eu-west-1 (example)
    • Event Bus Name: Test_CPI_Content
    • Source: s4hana
    • Detail Type: BusinessPartnerEvent
    • Credential Name: Reference to AWS credentials (stored securely in SAP CI)
    • Event Detail: The JSON body prepared in step 2

For more information about Amazon EventBridge Receiver adapter configurations, please visit this link. Amazon EventBridge Receiver Adapter

Step 4: Testing
Once the iFlow is deployed, create or change a Business Partner in SAP S/4HANA. Once it is done, you will be able to see a message triggered from SAP S/4 HANA and coming into SAP CI iFlow. The iFlow will convert the XML data into JSON and sends it to Amazon EventBridge.
You will be able to see a corresponding message appear in the Amazon EventBridge Event Bus.
You can then route it to:

  • AWS Lambda to process or enrich data
  • Amazon SQS/SNS to notify other systems
  • Amazon Kinesis or Data Lake for analytics

End-to-end Process from SAP ERP to Amazon EventBridge

Below is the process in which a business partner is created in S4 HANA and sent to SAP Integration Suite. SAP Integration Suite transforms the incoming message into JSON and sends it to Amazon SQS(Simple Queue Service) using Amazon Eventbridge receiver adapter. 

  1. New Business Partner in SAP S4Hana

Business Partner ‘12345’ gets created in SAP S4 Hana 

  1. Received message in SAP CI

SAP Integration Suite received the message from S4Hana. IFlow converts incoming XML payload into JSON and sent to AWS using Amazon Eventbridge receiver adapter. 

XML Payload received from Sap S/4 Hana:

<?xml version='1.0' encoding='UTF-8'?><root><type>sap.s4.beb.businesspartner.v1.BusinessPartner_Changed_v1</type>
<datacontenttype>application/json</datacontenttype><specversion>1.0</specversion><source>...</source><id>c051226f-59a8-1edd-bef6-7efcb8aaffc</id><time>2024-01-11T12:56:29Z</time><data><BusinessPartner>12345</BusinessPartner></data></root>

JSON payload sent to Amazon EventBridge after transformation:

{

  "root": {

    "type": "sap.s4.beb.businesspartner.v1.BusinessPartner_Changed_v1",

    "datacontenttype": "application/json",

    "specversion": "1.0",

    "source": "...",

    "id": "c051226f-59a8-1edd-bef6-7efcb8aaffc",

    "time": "2024-01-11T12:56:29Z",

    "data": {

      "BusinessPartner": "12345"

    }

  }

}
  1. Business Partner Details replicated in Amazon EventBridge

Amazon Event Bus details

Put event is recorded in Amazon Eventbridge.

Data sent into Amazon SQS

The above processing took less than 1 second to complete end-to-end. This ensures near real-time processing of data which makes further processing easier and faster.

Benefits of Using the Amazon EventBridge Adapter

  • Real-Time Integration: From SAP to Amazon EventBridge, the data will be transferred in near real time, which will make integrations much faster.
  • Event-Driven Architecture: Promotes usage of decoupled system interactions and better scalability.
  • Simplified Configuration: Eliminates the need for intermediate APIs or custom connectors. This ensures simplification of complex integrations.
  • Serverless and Cost-Effective: Uses AWS’s serverless infrastructure with pay-per-event pricing.
  • Time Saving: Takes very less time in configurations and implementation.

Best Practices

  • Use consistent naming conventions for event types and sources.
  • In case of failures, retry mechanisms should be implemented to avoid data loss.
  • Leverage SAP Cloud Integration monitoring to track message processing status.
  • Use AWS CloudWatch for monitoring and auditing event delivery.
  • Maintain version control of your integration artifacts to track changes and ensure reliability.
  • Use batching mechanism in case of large number of messages for smooth working.

Conclusion

The Amazon EventBridge receiver adapter extends the capabilities of SAP Integration Suite to support modern, event-driven integrations between SAP and AWS platforms.
By using this adapter, organizations can replicate business partner data, sales order data, purchase order data and other master or transactional data from SAP S/4HANA to AWS in real time which can be further used for processing or analytics. Also, Amazon EventBridge adapter can be used to send multiple events using advanced mode settings in the adapter. This functionality helps in complex event-driven architectures saving good amount of time.

This approach provides a scalable, secure, time saving and future-ready integration pattern that aligns with cloud-native architectures and digital transformation goals.

Hope you find this blog informative!

Thanks

Rupali

entry.content:

Thanks,


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: My S-user ID will expire, how can I extend the expira...
entry.content:
SAP Support cannot extend the expiration date of S-user IDs. Please contact one of your super administrators for your customer number to extend the expiration date of your S-user ID. You can find a list of administrators on the My Important Contacts.

Or in SAP for Me My Settings, click Is Your S-User ID About to Expire?. This will take you to the My Important Contacts page to see a pre-filtered list of administrators you can contact to update your S-user ID.

Please refer to following SAP KBA's for more information:

2971742 - How to extend the Expiration Date of your own S-user ID - SAP for Me

2930885 - How administrators manage the Expiration Date for other S-user IDs - SAP for Me

entry.title: Re: FORM development in sales order to get the AVC charac...
entry.content:
Good day @arturo_suarez 

Thank you for your question.

The reason you cannot find the CDS views you mentioned is because they are not released in public cloud. That means you cannot bind them directly in your form.

Instead, I suggest you:

  1. Check if the AVC data you require is exposed via a released API/CDS in your system. The Business Hub and View Browser can be very handy for that.
  2. If you do not find them, consider using a supported output enhancement BAdI to enrich your form data.

My recommendation is that, for now, you should design your form with released sales document data only, then add AVC value only through a released enhancement point. Avoid building an unreleased CDS.

Consult the references below for more insights.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/aff0b3f5f46c42a2b2c0fabfc233bab2/65f5fd6ac102431fab0974c19c24f220.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/87f9b54f9c4f4e75aff0061860a6589a/c70d06ed39f34108b520e85dcc7de0c5.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/6ef7f849fed34f95adfc449f29835255/a5b52dc9406f4cadadd7eca6578bbee5.html?locale=en-US

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: SAP Service and Asset Manager Phase model Work Orders
entry.content:
Hi,
The Phase Model process normally starts with a 'maintenance request' , so a 'notification' in SSAM.
If you want to turn this into a Work Order immediately, skipping some of the intermediate phases, look into the creation of a notification 'with emergency processing context'. When you set this in SSAM, a Work Order will be created locally and be 'ready for execution'.
S/4 help guide on processing context: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/e72f747389b340229f7fa343975bfa57/c9e3772f8ded4db4b91c14ce72bf57c3.html
SSAM Config: https://help.sap.com/docs/service-asset-manager/sap-service-and-asset-manager-configuration-guide/emergency-or-minor-work?version=LATEST&locale=en-US
Hope this helps.
D.

entry.title: Re: BAPI_SHIPMENT_CHANGE clean core alternative?
entry.content:
Hi sh4il3sh

Normally I would reply that all BAPIs are released, so I was curious why you say this function is unreleased.

It seems that SAP has build a real pitfall with this function because they have named it BAPI_...., but it is not a BAPI. All BAPIs should exist in transaction BAPI but there is no BAPI for shipment change - only for shipment creation.

This is in my opinion a serious error from SAP, so I searched the SAP Note base in me.sap.com. I didn't find anything useful, but the AI in me.sap.com gave this suggestion to use the IDOC interface, so that is a clean core alternative:

You are inquiring about the BAPI_SHIPMENT_CHANGE function module. This function module is not released for customers, meaning there is no available function module to update a shipment document directly through this BAPI. To address the need to update a shipment document without using standard transactions, you can follow these steps: 1. Understand the Limitation: Recognize that BAPI_SHIPMENT_CHANGE is not available for updating shipment documents in the standard design. 2. Use Alternative Method: Instead of using the BAPI, utilize the IDoc interface to change shipment documents. The specific function module to use is IDOC_INPUT_SHPMNT. 3. Implement IDoc:

  • Prepare the necessary data for the shipment document you wish to update.
  • Create an IDoc with the relevant segments that correspond to the shipment data.
  • Call the function module IDOC_INPUT_SHPMNT to process the IDoc and update the shipment document in the system.

4. Monitor the Process: After executing the IDoc, check for any errors or issues in the processing logs to ensure that the shipment document has been updated successfully. 5. Testing: It is advisable to test the IDoc processing in a development or quality assurance environment before applying it in production. 💡 Pro Tip(s):

  • Ensure that the IDoc structure matches the expected format for the shipment document to avoid processing errors.
  • Regularly monitor the IDoc processing status to catch any issues early.

Best Regards 
Thomas Madsen Nielsen

entry.title: Re: BAPI_SHIPMENT_CHANGE clean core alternative?
entry.content:
@TMNielsen @sh4il3sh Why would you really need to replace BAPI_SHIPMENT_CHANGE? Even though it is not released and as per note 2825900 - Released Function Modules to update SD shipment documents 

the BAPI is still the best option if you need synchronous processing. IDoc processing as suggested by SAP is asynchronous. 

LE-TRA is already obsolete and on the compatibility pack. Why would you worry about BAPI_SHIPMENT_CHANGE, when the entire LE-TRA needs to be replaced?

entry.title: Re: Italian special depreciation keys for ledger 2L
entry.content:
The depreciation key is created under the chart of depreciation. The chart of depreciation can set up two Depreciation Areas (one for GAAP and the other for IFRS) and post to two parallel ledgers respectively (the main ledger and the L2 ledger).

Is this what you want to achieve? In my personal opinion, there is no problem with this.

entry.title: Re: Crystal Report 2025 EOL
entry.content:
Hi,

Currently, SAP Crystal Reports 2025 has an End of Mainstream Maintenance (EOMM) date of December 2027, and as of now SAP has not announced any Extended Support beyond that date.

Any post-2027 support would depend on future SAP roadmap decisions, but there is no confirmed extended maintenance program at this time.

Given this relatively short support horizon, many organizations are starting to evaluate modern reporting alternatives such as:

  • Microsoft Power BI / Microsoft Fabric reporting
  • Cloud-native BI platforms with longer product lifecycles
  • Centralized data warehouse/lakehouse-based reporting tools

If long-term stability and innovation are priorities, it may be worth considering a modernization roadmap rather than another Crystal Reports upgrade.

entry.title: Configure certificate auth for Microsoft Sentinel with S/4HANA Cloud public edition
entry.content:
Configure client certificate authentication for Microsoft Sentinel Solution for SAP integration with S/4HANA Cloud public edition

Quick link to GitHub.

For many SAP S/4HANA Cloud public edition APIs basic authentication is the default. SAP recommends client certificate use for production tenants.

This article shows you how to use client certificate authentication with your Microsoft Sentinel Solution for SAP integration. Security Audit Log API serves as an example. Approach applies to any of your APIs governed by communication arrangements.

 

How It Works

Instead of the native connector – which is limited to Basic Auth – choose the Sentinel for SAP extension package on SAP Integration Suite for full flexibility.

  1. Create Communication Scenario SAP_COM_0750 the usual way.
  2. Create a communication user for certificate authentication and upload your certificate. The built-in cert sap_cloudintegrationcertificate provided by every SAP Cloud Integration tenant is supported out-of-the-box for ease of use. For custom Client Certificates learn more from SAP's blog by @marc_roeder and ensure that the certificate signing authority is trusted by SAP. Find more details on SAP Note 2801396.

3. Configure a destination for your S/4HANA Cloud public edition tenant and set authentication to ClientCertificateAuthentication.

 

Property

Value

Description

Name

S4-PC-[SID]-[Client]

Destination name (e.g., S4-PC-YKJ-100)

Type

HTTP

Connection type

URL

https://[tenant]-api.s4hana.cloud.sap

S/4HANA Cloud system API URL

Proxy Type

Internet

Always internet because of the cloud nature of the SAP service

Authentication

ClientCertificateAuthentication

Authentication methods supported by S/4HANA Cloud public edition

Key Store Source

ClientProvided

this will be used as trigger for the iflow to use X509

Key Store Location

(empty)

not applicable

Key Store Password

(empty)

not applicable

This setting is evaluated during runtime on the iFlow. See below Screenshot for reference:

 

4. Deploy the latest Microsoft Sentinel extension package in SAP Integration Suite. See this video for a similar scenario for guidance. Ignore the ERP specific settings on Cloud Connector etc. They don’t apply to public cloud.

 

  1. Deploy the agentless data connector for the Microsoft Sentinel Solution for SAP. See this video for a walk-through of the first steps and the official Microsoft Learn page here. Ignore the ERP specific settings on Cloud Connector etc. They don’t apply to public cloud.
  2. Connect your new iFlow on the data connector pane on Sentinel to start ingesting SAP S/4HANA Cloud public edition logs. On the Advanced section supply the path “/community/ SAPS4_Public_Cloud_Security_Audit_Log” to point the route at the S4 public cloud iFlow on SAP Integration Suite.

 

Observe the messages flowing on SAP Cloud Integration monitoring and Microsoft Sentinel for SAP.

You can use the following kusto query on Azure Log Analytics or Defender portal to verify the S4 logs. Filter by SystemId in case you have multiple connections:

SAPAuditLog
| where SystemId == "your SID"

Continue your onboarding with Analytic Rules

Both SAP’s native connector and the integration suite based approach post data to the SAPAuditLog structures in Sentinel. This way the built-in security content for the private cloud solution is automatically lit up for public cloud too.

Be aware that detections for legacy interfaces such as RFC are not applicable anymore because they are disabled in public cloud tenants.

Final Words

That's a wrap 🎬 you saw today how to elevate the security of your integration of S/4HANA Cloud public edition with Microsoft Sentinel Solution for SAP using client certificate authentication. Good job!

Cherry on the cake: You can save some maintenance by using the pre-provided certificate of SAP Cloud Integration. SAP takes care of renewal. Only remaining task is to update the communication user on S4. This API allows full automation of that step even. See this blog for details on the automatic refresh approach in a similar scenario.

Quick link to GitHub.

Feel free to reach out to talk more SAP Security.

Cheers, Martin

entry.title: Re: Actual Overhead Calculation for Projects (BNA) - No E...
entry.content:
Hello,

Could you please check whether a costing sheet has been assigned to your project? You can find this under the Control tab in the app: Project Control > Enterprise Projects > F3215. Assigning a costing sheet is required if you want to run the overhead calculation.

We support two types of costing sheets:

  • Event-based: Overhead is calculated automatically during cost postings, so you do not need to run the overhead calculation separately.
  • Periodic: You must run the overhead calculation manually (single processing) or schedule it as a job.

Here you can find more details about costing sheet including some FAQ section. Costing Sheet | SAP Help Portal

Best regards,

Katerina

entry.title: Delivery Integration in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Transportation Management
entry.content:
Delivery Integration in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Transportation Management

Delivery-Based Freight Units 

1. Business Background 

To simplify your Freight Order process in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Transportation Management, you can configure delivery-based Freight Unit (FU) creation. Once sales orders and deliveries exist, Freight Units are created automatically by the system. Planning can start immediately. 

This blog post is part of the ‘Simplified Freight Order’ series, which can be found here: https://community.sap.com/t5/blogs/blogworkflowpage/blog-id/scm-blog-sap/article-id/8018 
 
Process Comparison 
Other that the default process, in the delivery based approach the delivery automatically triggers freight unit (FU) creation, streamlining planning and reducing manual effort. 

  • Standard process: Sales Order → Freight Unit → Plan on Freight Order → Create Deliveries (…) 
  • Delivery based process: Sales Order → Delivery → Freight Unit -> Plan Freight Order (…) 

2. Configuration  

  • Documentation: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/61e246f4b34c4e1790d8b7651c0b40a8/08069648fa1f42caa0fc1a4ba5a69d6e.html?locale=en-US&state=DRAFT 

 2.1 Step-by-Step Setup 

  • Prerequisite: Log in with a user assigned the BPC_EXPERT role. 
  • Open the ‘Manage Your Solution’ app. 

  • Navigate to ‘Transportation Order Management’ 

  • Navigate to step 6 ‘Define Transportation-Relevance of Delivery Documents’ 

  • Maintain the following values for ‘Define Transportation-Relevance of Delivery Documents’ 
  • Relevant shipping point 
  • Delivery type: Outbound Delivery 
  • Shipping condition: TM internal/external planning 
  • Control key: Integrate Outbound Delivery 
  • Logistics integration profile: Planning Cloud Outbound 

  • Navigate to step 3 ‘Define Logistics Integration Profile’ 

  • Verify that the correct Freight Unit Building Rule is maintained for the Logistics Integration profile assigned in the previous step. 

  • Save your changes 

2.2 Create and Validate Transactions 

  • Create a sales order and verify planning-relevant fields: 
  • Incoterms: set to a planning-relevant Incoterm. 
  • Ship-to, shipping condition, and shipping point align with your TM integration settings. 
  • Create the delivery and confirm: 
  • A Freight Unit is created automatically. 
  • The FU is visible in the Transportation Cockpit and ready for assignment to a Freight Order. 

 

Further Information

If you are interested in further automation capabilities in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Transportation Management check the complete blog post series:
https://community.sap.com/t5/blogs/blogworkflowpage/blog-id/scm-blog-sap/article-id/8018

entry.title: Simplified Transportation Cockpit in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Transportation Management
entry.content:
Simplified Transportation Cockpit in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Transportation Management

1. Overview

1.1 Purpose and Benefits 

  • Streamline daily planning by focusing the Transportation Cockpit on the data and actions you use most. 
  • Reduce visual noise, speed up decision-making, and make the cockpit easier to adopt for lightweight logistics. 

1.2 Configuration Tasks

  • Custom selection criteria (saved searches) 
  • A custom cockpit view (visible columns and order) 
  • Saved section views so your layout persists 

2. Custom Selection Criteria Configuration

  • Open the Transportation Cockpit and set the filters you use most often.
  • Collapse criteria that aren’t relevant to your daily planning to keep the filter panel clean. 
  • Save the filter set as a custom search so you can reuse it.

 3. Custom Cockpit View Configuration

  • Click the settings icon. If it’s not visible, open the menu behind the three dots and select the settings option. 
  • Choose which columns to show and arrange their order to highlight the fields you need for planning. 
  • Hide non-essential columns to reduce scrolling and distraction. 
  • Save the view by clicking the arrow in each section so the layout is retained 

4. Good Practices 

  • Keep filters minimal and name saved searches clearly (for example, by lane, carrier, or plant). 
  • Align the column set with your automation approach (e.g., show statuses and fields you use to trigger or monitor automated planning and execution). 
  • Review and update the view periodically as your process evolves. 

 

5. Further Information

  • Transportation cockpit documentation in SAP Help Portal
    https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/61e246f4b34c4e1790d8b7651c0b40a8/54deed6bbd8047e28dfa219370b7832c.html  

  • If you are interested in further automation capabilities in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Transportation Management check the complete blog post series:
    https://community.sap.com/t5/blogs/blogworkflowpage/blog-id/scm-blog-sap/article-id/8018

entry.title: Event Driven TM in S/4HANA Public Cloud: Update Delivery Data When a Freight Order is created
entry.content:
Event Driven TM in S/4HANA Public Cloud: Update Delivery Data When a Freight Order is created 

1. Overview 

If you repeat simple updates in Transportation Management, such as setting general data on every Freight Order, it is time to automate them. When the decision is straightforward and the action is always the same, automation saves time and avoids errors. 

This series shows you how to build a small, event-driven extension in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition. In our example, when a Freight Order is created, a local event handler updates static header data automatically. 

1.1 Why this pattern 

  • Cuts manual effort for routine Freight Order updates 
  • Uses released cloud APIs (usage type C1) and ABAP logic 
  • Upgrade-safe and simple to maintain 

Example we will build 

  • Trigger: Freight Order created 
  • Action: Update general header data with predefined values 
  • Result: Each Freight Order starts with consistent, ready-to-use data 

 1.2. Prerequisites  

  • Developer Extensibility (3-system landscape) is enabled 
  • The required view or API is released for ABAP Cloud (usage type C1) 
  • ABAP Development Tools (ADT) are installed and connected to your system 
  • You have a development package in the customer namespace. 
  • You have access to create classes your development environment.

 1.3 Links 

  • Stability Contract for System-Internal Use (C1): https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/c0c54048d35849128be8e872df5bea6d/b6fe6846e9c7488e8c7e6d4289918fe3.html?locale=en-US  
  • Local consumption of businees events 
    https://help.sap.com/docs/abap-cloud/abap-rap/business-event-consumption?version=s4hana_cloud&locale=en-US  
  • CDS Views for Freight Order Management: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/c0c54048d35849128be8e872df5bea6d/125ff931b6274419b2164211920c74e3.html?locale=en-US  

 

 1.4 Alternatives 

There is more than one way to automate routine tasks in Transportation Management. The local event consumption approach is ideal for simple, immediate updates. For more complex needs, consider: 

  • Job scheduling: periodic, time based updates or high volume corrections 
  • BAdIs: governed logic at standard enhancement points during process steps 

Choose the option that best fits your timing, volume, and control requirements. 

2. Implementation Steps 

2.1 Find relevant event in ADT 

You need the behavior-enabled object for Freight Order, not just a read-only CDS view. The correct BDEF for this example is I_FreightOrderTP. 

Why I_FreightOrderTP? 

  • TP stands for transactional processing. TP interface views are behavior-enabled. 
  • Events, actions, validations, and determinations are defined in the RAP behavior definition (BDEF) behind the TP view. 

Plain interface views such as I_TransportationOrder_2 are read-only and do not expose events. 

Finding the BDEF in ADT 

  • In ADT, use ABAP Repository Search to open the BDEF entity I_FreightOrderTP. 
  • In the BDEF, look for ‘use event‘ declarations. 
  • You subscribe to the alias. In our exampe ‘Created’ 

2.2 Create the Implementation Class 

2.2.1 Global Class 

  • Create a new ABAP class in the customer namespace. 
  • In the class definition, declare the relevant business event using the FOR EVENTS OF keyword so the class can receive the event. 
  • Do not implement business logic in the global class.

2.2.2 Local Class Implementation 

  • Go to the tab ‘Local Types’ tab. 
  • Create a local handler that receives the event data and performs the update. 
  • Use EML to update the Freight Order entity. Keep the update focused and transactional. 
  •  

2.2.3 Example: Enrich the Freight Order 

In this example, you update: 

  • Purchasing Organization 
  • Purchasing Group 
  • Carrier 

Use static data to start. This proves the flow endtoend quickly. Once validated, introduce simple business rules. For example: 

  • Derive Carrier based on Shipping Point (e.g., map shipping points to preferred carriers). 
  • Apply fallback logic if no match is found (e.g., set a default carrier and log a warning). 

2.2.4 What’s Next 

  • Next, test the event flow endtoend: trigger the event, verify the updates via EML, and confirm the business outcome in the Freight Order. 

2.2.5 Tips and Pitfalls

  • Business events run as separate transaction, asynchronously after the main transaction. It might be necessary to refresh the UI to reflect the changes. If you need to make changes withing the main LUW, consider using BADI instead.
  • Consistency: Use correct business terms (“Freight Order,” “Purchasing Organization,” “Purchasing Group,” “Carrier”). 
  • Robustness: Validate event payloads, and handle missing or inconsistent data 
  • Performance: Keep event processing light; avoid longrunning operations in the handler. 
  • Testability: Write unit tests for the local handler logic where possible. 

 

3. Code Snippet: Event Handler to Default Carrier and Purchasing Data 

3.1 What this does 

  • Listens to the “Freight Order created” event and defaults carrier, purchasing organization and purchasing group.
  • Uses local business event consumption and EML updates against released RAP business objects.

3.2 Before you start 

  • This sample is provided asis. Use at your own risk and verify in a test tenant. 
  • Adapt constants and namespaces to your customer namespace. 
  • Only use C1 released objects. 
  • Verify which attributes you can use to differentiate which freight orders should be processed . 

3.3 Global Class

Create a global class in the customer namespace without any method implementations.

CLASS <classname_customer_namespace> DEFINITION PUBLIC ABSTRACT FINAL FOR EVENTS OF I_FreightOrderTP.
   PUBLIC SECTION.
   PROTECTED SECTION.
   PRIVATE SECTION.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS <classname_customer_namespace> IMPLEMENTATION.
ENDCLASS.

 

3.4 Local Class

Open tab local types and create the class implementation. Keep in mind, this is only a template and the coding needs to be adjusted to match your organisations requirements.

CLASS lhe_<classname_customer_namespace> DEFINITION INHERITING FROM cl_abap_behavior_event_handler.

  PRIVATE SECTION.
    METHODS on_created FOR ENTITY EVENT newlycreatedfreightorder FOR freightorder~created.
ENDCLASS.


CLASS lhe_<classname_customer_namespace> IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD on_created.
    CONSTANTS lc_purchasing_organization TYPE /scmtms/vdm_pur_org_ext_id VALUE <purchase_org>.
    CONSTANTS lc_purchasing_group TYPE /scmtms/vdm_pur_grp_ext_id VALUE <purchase_group>.
    CONSTANTS lc_carrier_uuid TYPE /scmtms/vdm_carrier_party_key VALUE <carrier_uuid>.

    LOOP AT newlycreatedfreightorder ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<singlefreightorder>).
      IF <singlefreightorder>-Carrier IS NOT INITIAL.
        CONTINUE.
      ENDIF.

    MODIFY ENTITY i_freightordertp
      UPDATE FROM VALUE #( ( %key-TransportationOrderUUID = <singlefreightorder>-   TransportationOrderUUID
        TranspPurgOrgExtID = lc_purchasing_organization
        TranspPurgGroupExtID = lc_purchasing_group
        CarrierUUID = lc_carrier_uuid
        %control-TranspPurgOrgExtID = cl_abap_behv=>flag_changed
        %control-TranspPurgGroupExtID = cl_abap_behv=>flag_changed
        %control-CarrierUUID = cl_abap_behv=>flag_changed ) )
      FAILED DATA(ls_create_failed)
      REPORTED DATA(ls_create_reported).
    ENDLOOP.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

 

3.5 Risk Reduction Checklist for Productive Usage

  • Validate before update: Ensure the Freight Order exists and is updatable before issuing EML.
  • Handle errors: Catch and log EML failures with clear message categories; surface actionable context.
  • Consider BRFR+ tables instead of hard-coding any values
  • Keep it light. Consider job processing or BADI implementation for more complex logic.

4. Conclusion

  • Automating routine Freight Order updates via a local event handler in S/4HANA Cloud ensures consistent, upgrade-safe data with minimal manual effort.
  • If you are interested in further automation capabilities in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Transportation Management check the complete blog post series:
    https://community.sap.com/t5/blogs/blogworkflowpage/blog-id/scm-blog-sap/article-id/8018 

 

Thanks to my co-author @Slava_Aliakseyevets

entry.title: Re: EWM Public Cloud - Change warehouse address in qualit...
entry.content:
Good day @emmanuelm1 

Thank you for your question.

Your reading is correct; it is a local configuration. You are required to use SSCUI 102657 to maintain it manually in each target system and not via transport. Note that you cannot transport this entry from CBC/DEV to Q/P.

The best approach is to log in to your tenant, open the configure your solution app, search for the SSCUI mentioned above, open activity and maintain your warehouse address and save it. Do same for your Prod. Tenant.

If 102657 is not visible in Q/P, ask your admin to assign the configuration role/catalog for Expert Configuration in your tenants.

See the following references for more insights:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/f369b2eff700401494ba6e7c9a573288/ffb815dc188144b8b14f8c19929edba1.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2bba750d1e124e1ea2a039bb1cd9b6c5/9d750d3c01764058b970cd39d16ec9bc.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/f369b2eff700401494ba6e7c9a573288/38ed6778530c49ef87bb776a92436971.html?locale=en-US

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris
