entry.title: Re: Will there be support for Crystal Reports Runtime 64...
entry.content:
Hi Clemens,

This is a very relevant question, and we’re seeing similar concerns from organizations currently using SAP Crystal Reports (developer version for Microsoft Visual Studio), particularly where it’s embedded within business applications.

With long-term support beyond 2027 still a consideration, many teams are proactively:
• Reviewing lifecycle and dependency risks
• Evaluating modernization options such as Power BI, Microsoft Fabric, or embedded analytics alternatives
• Planning phased migration strategies to reduce technical debt

It may be worth beginning roadmap discussions early to avoid last-minute platform pressure.

If helpful, I’d be glad to connect offline and share how other organizations are approaching post-2027 planning.

entry.title: New POD 2.0 Framework: Template-Based Design Instead of POD Types
entry.content:
This blog post is part of a series on SAP Digital Manufacturing new POD 2.0 framework, the new generation of SAP Digital Manufacturing worker UIs.

The next post on the series will be published as soon as this one reaches 20 Likes, so please leave a Like! 👍

🗓️ Timeline

POD 2.0 is currently in BETA with selected customers, General Availability (GA) is planned for 2605 release. BETA content is subject to change!

📢 Creating PODs 2.0

To create a new POD 2.0 during BETA phase, you must be enrolled to the BETA program.
Then,  ensure that you have the DMC_BETA_APPS role so you can see and access Manage PODs 2.0 app, and then click 'Create'.

Then, select a POD Template, ender POD ID and Description, Icon URL, select Density and Session Timeout value and click 'OK'.

📢 Template-Based Design Instead of POD Types

One of the biggest architectural shifts in POD 2.0 is the move from rigid POD types to a template-based design approach.

In the previous generation, you had to choose a predefined PDO type, work center, order, operation, and so on. That decision dictated what capabilities were available and often forced trade-offs. Template-based design removes that limitation.

Each Template comes with a number of preconfigured plugins that are related to the type of manufacturing process the Template is named after. Choosing the Template that's most closely related to your manufacturing process can potentially save you the extra effort needed to configure a Blank POD from scratch.

Work Center POD Template 🔗

By creating a POD using the Work Center POD Template, you can leverage its preconfigured pages and dialogs that are designed to enable production operators to support the execution of production orders and service orders on the shop floor. Each preconfigured page and dialog comes with preconfigured plugins that organize the layout of the POD and add relevant functionality.

Operation Activity POD Template 🔗

By creating a POD using the Operation Activity POD Template, you can leverage its preconfigured pages and dialogs that are designed to enable production operators to support the execution of production and service orders, and to queue SFCs at specific operation activities in the manufacturing process. Each preconfigured page and dialog comes with preconfigured plugins that organize the layout and add relevant functionality.

Order POD Template 🔗

By creating a POD using the Order POD Template, you can leverage its preconfigured pages and dialogs that are designed to enable production operators to support the execution of process and production orders that are transferred from integrated ERP systems. Each preconfigured page and dialog comes with preconfigured plugins that organize the layout and add relevant functionality.

Note that Consume Component POD Plugin is now harmonized between Work Center and Order PODs.

Electronic Batch Record POD Template 🔗

By creating a POD using the Electronic Batch Record POD Template, you can leverage its preconfigured pages and dialogs that are designed to enable, for example, regulated industries to support the composition of electronic batch records related to process orders on the shop floor. This includes the flexibility to add both SAP Digital Manufacturing documents as well as third party files to the EBR to compose a comprehensive batch record. Each preconfigured page and dialog comes with preconfigured plugins that organize the layout of the POD and add relevant functionality.

I will cover Electronic Batch Record (EBR) functionality in detail in the upcoming blog posts of this series, stay tuned.

After creating and saving your brand-new POD, there are a number of next steps you can take. The following subsections describe the main ones:

Launch Your POD

When you want to launch and run your POD, choose  'View your POD in a new tab'. Your POD opens in a new tab in your browser.

Copy the Configuration of Your POD

When you want to duplicate the configuration of your own POD and be able save it as a new POD, choose  'Copy the configuration of your current POD into a new POD'.

Edit the Settings of Your POD

When you want to change the settings of your new POD, such as the ID, Version, Description, Icon URL, Content Density, or the Session Timeout, choose 'Change the settings of your POD' to open the POD Settings dialog, where you can edit the settings of your POD.

The next post on the series will be published as soon as this one reaches 20 Likes, so please leave a Like! 👍

Stay tuned for more! Thanks,
Manoel Costa

entry.title: Creating Custom Competencies in SAP SuccessFactors Performance Management via TIH
entry.content:
Introduction                                           

SAP SuccessFactors has been steadily moving toward Talent Intelligence Hub (TIH) as the single framework for managing attributes such as competencies, skills, behaviors, and traits. If you’re implementing or migrating Performance Management, understanding how to create and use custom competencies via TIH is now essential.

This blog walks through the prerequisites, end-to-end configuration steps to create and use a custom competency in Performance Management.

What is Talent Intelligence Hub (TIH)?

Talent Intelligence Hub is SuccessFactors’ unified data model for talent attributes. Competencies created in TIH can be reused consistently across modules such as: SP&CDP, PMGM, RCM and Opportunity Marketplace.

Prerequisites:

Before you start configuration, ensure the following prerequisites are met:

  • You've migrated to Talent Intelligence Hub.
  • Ensure the administrator has access to:
    Admin Center, Manage Talent Intelligence Hub, Manage Competencies and Skills (if available), Manage Permission Roles etc.

Configuration

Step 1: Navigate to the Attributes Library

  1. Go to Admin Center → Manage Talent Intelligence Hub.
  2. Open the Attributes Library, which stores all Skills, Competencies, and Custom Attributes.
           
            

       3. Tag - Create tags

 

          

Here, you will be able to:

  • Create new attributes
  • Define attribute types
  • Set proficiency scales
  • Add behaviors if needed

Step 2: Create a New Custom Competency

  1. Click Create → Attribute.

  2. Select Attribute Type = Competency.

  3. Add:

    • Name of the competency
    • Description
    • Behaviors (optional, but recommended)
    • Tags such as Core, Critical, or custom tags
      (Tags help classify competencies for filtering and auto-population.)
                         

 

        4. Set the Proficiency Level Scale, which defines how the competency will be rated in                        performance forms.
 
Once saved, your new competency becomes part of the centralized TIH library.
 

Step 3: (Optional but Recommended) Map the Custom Competency to Job Roles

If you want the competency to auto-populate in the employee’s Growth Portfolio or appear in role-based performance reviews:

  1. Navigate to Manage Job Profile Content.
  2. Select a Job Role.
  3. Open Mapped Attributes.
  4. Add your newly created custom competency.
    Mapping competencies to job roles ensures they cascade automatically into the employee’s profile.
This is particularly useful if your competency is job-specific.

Step 4: Add the Custom Competency to Performance Forms

       1. Go to Manage Templates and select a template

       2. Edit Fields and Sections > Competency field

       3. Check the "Custom" Box

           

       4.Select "Show advanced options..." and scroll down to the bottom

       5. Choose "0 Custom Competency defined. Click to modify."

       6. Select which competencies to include

             

         7. Once selected, the custom competencies will be listed

These competencies will populate on all newly launched forms from this template.

Note: You can only add custom competencies via XML configuration of your template. Reference XML is provided in Custom competencies | Talent Intelligent Hub | SAP Help Portal 

Conclusion

With SAP’s strategic shift toward the Talent Intelligence Hub, using TIH as the central source for managing competencies is no longer optional—it’s becoming foundational for future‑ready talent processes. By creating competencies directly in TIH, organizations gain a unified, reusable, and scalable competency model that supports multiple modules across the SuccessFactors landscape.

The steps covered in this blog—from creating competencies in the Attributes Library, mapping them to job roles, and integrating them into performance form templates—ensure that custom competencies are configured correctly and consistently across the system. This approach not only streamlines Performance Management setup but also enhances data accuracy, talent insights, and employee development alignment.

Adopting TIH today sets the stage for more intelligent, connected, and insight‑driven talent experiences across your SAP SuccessFactors environment.

entry.title: ADT Navigation through open tabs
entry.content:
ADT in Eclipse: Is it just me, or has the Ctrl+PageUp/Down shortcut not been working properly for months? As soon as I install the ADTs in Eclipse, navigation through the open tabs is inverted. And if you encounter an ABAP class while navigating, it breaks completely. This means that you can only switch back and forth between the class and the adjacent tab, and in the class tab, you slip into the Test Classes sub-tab.

I've been experiencing this behavior for many months now and thought it would eventually go away. But it hasn't. Even with a new Eclipse instance and a new workspace.
